title: aquilegia vulgaris subsp. viscosa
intro: Aquilegia vulgaris subsp. viscosa, also known as the sticky or viscid columbine, is a plant species within the Aquilegia genus, characterized by its bright purple flowers and sticky or glandular leaves. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal effects. The health benefits of Aquilegia vulgaris subsp. viscosa are said to include the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential use as a natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. The plant's medicinal properties can be obtained through various herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although it's essential to consult a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment for any health condition.
